sourceTitle: How to join your team that’s using Notion

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of the sidebar in a Notion team workspace?

options.text: It displays recent notifications only.
options.explanation: The sidebar does more than show notifications; it holds teamspaces and top‑level pages.

options.text: It holds teamspaces with top‑level pages, the highest‑level homes for information.
options.explanation: Correct – the sidebar is where teamspaces and their top‑level pages live, providing a navigation hub.

options.text: It automatically creates new databases for each user.
options.explanation: Databases must be created manually; the sidebar does not auto‑generate them.

options.text: It serves as a chat window for team members.
options.explanation: Chat occurs in comments or mentions, not in the sidebar itself.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT listed as a type of content block in Notion?

options.text: Callout block
options.explanation: Callout blocks are explicitly mentioned as a type of content block.

options.text: Video
options.explanation: Videos are listed among the possible block types.

options.text: Spreadsheet
options.explanation: The article does not list a "spreadsheet" block; Notion uses databases for tabular data.

options.text: Text
options.explanation: Text is one of the basic block types described.

questions.question: How does the Quick Find functionality help you in a Notion workspace?

options.text: It automatically shares the page you’re viewing with the whole team.
options.explanation: Quick Find is for searching, not for sharing.

options.text: It locates a particular document or database quickly.
options.explanation: Correct – Quick Find lets you search for specific docs or databases.

options.text: It creates a new template based on the page you’re on.
options.explanation: Creating templates is a separate action; Quick Find does not generate templates.

options.text: It sends a notification to all team members about the page you opened.
options.explanation: Quick Find does not send notifications; it only helps you find content.

questions.question: Where will you see a notification when someone mentions you in a Notion workspace?

options.text: In the top of the sidebar, in your Inbox.
options.explanation: Correct – mentions appear in your Inbox, located near the top of the sidebar.

options.text: In the footer of the page you’re viewing.
options.explanation: Mentions are not displayed in the page footer.

options.text: In a separate email sent by Notion.
options.explanation: While email notifications can be enabled, the article specifically mentions the Inbox.

options.text: In the Quick Find results list.
options.explanation: Quick Find shows search results, not notification alerts.

questions.question: Which statement about page sharing in a team workspace is accurate?

options.text: All pages are automatically shared with every team member.
options.explanation: The article notes that not all pages are shared with everyone; sharing settings can be specific.

options.text: Specific sharing settings can be applied so that only certain members can view a page.
options.explanation: Correct – pages can have custom sharing permissions limiting access.

options.text: Sharing settings are only available for databases, not regular pages.
options.explanation: Both regular pages and databases can have sharing settings.

options.text: Once a page is shared, its permissions cannot be changed.
options.explanation: Permissions can be updated at any time; they are not permanent.

questions.question: How do the updated sidebar elements and teamspaces make sharing with the right groups easier?

options.text: Teamspaces group related top‑level pages together, allowing you to set sharing permissions at the teamspace level rather than on each individual page.
options.explanation: Correct – by organizing work into teamspaces, you can apply sharing settings to the whole space, simplifying permission management.

options.text: The sidebar now automatically shares every new page with the entire organization.
options.explanation: The update does not auto‑share everything; it provides better control, not blanket sharing.

options.text: Teamspaces replace the need for a sidebar entirely, removing any sharing options.
options.explanation: Teamspaces are an addition to the sidebar, not a removal, and sharing settings remain available.

options.text: Sharing is now handled exclusively through the Quick Find feature.
options.explanation: Quick Find is for searching, not for managing sharing permissions.

questions.question: Where can you browse the 10,000+ templates mentioned in the guide?

options.text: In the Notion mobile app’s Settings menu.
options.explanation: Templates are accessed via the web or desktop app, not specifically through the mobile Settings.

options.text: In the Template Gallery link provided in the guide.
options.explanation: Correct – the guide points to the Template Gallery where you can browse the templates.

options.text: By clicking the ‘Quick Find’ button and selecting ‘Templates’.
options.explanation: Quick Find searches for pages and databases; it does not directly open the Template Gallery.

options.text: Through the ‘Teamspaces’ section of the sidebar.
options.explanation: Teamspaces organize workspaces, not template browsing.
